what_this_pass_proved: Incident personality (unique dispatch lines and resolved lines per incident type) makes each call feel distinct — 'Kitchen fire on Oak Street' is more memorable than 'Incident requires 15'.
Dispatch status strip ('Standing by' → 'Calls stacking' → 'On fire') gives the player ambient situational awareness without adding a new UI element to track.
Visual warmth shift (cold blue to warm emergency tones) makes the station feel like a real place rather than a test screen.
open_questions: Do the personality lines get repetitive across sessions? May need a larger pool or randomization.
Does the status strip influence player behavior (dispatching faster when 'On fire') or is it just decoration?
Is the visual warmth noticeable enough to affect feel, or does it need stronger contrast?

bugs_fixed.description: All incidents used the same generic text. Added per-type dispatch and resolved lines with location and situation variety.
No ambient station state visible — player had no sense of overall pressure beyond individual incident timers. Added status strip.

known_limits: Personality line pool is fixed — not randomized across sessions. Repeat players will see the same lines.
Status strip thresholds (when it shifts from 'standing by' to 'calls stacking') have not been tuned against real play.
Visual warmth is a color palette shift only — no animation or ambient sound. May not be strong enough to change feel.
next_pass_first_obligation: Observe whether the personality and status strip make the player remember specific incidents or moments — if they describe 'that kitchen fire' instead of 'Level 3', the pass worked.
